title: The Chore Checklist
learningGoal: The learner will orally construct affirmative and negative Present Perfect statements on demand, matching 'have/has' auxiliaries to their respective pronouns while producing the correct irregular V3 phonology.
instructions: You are talking to your roommate, Amit. You are checking off a list of chores. Answer him ALOUD. Make sure you use the 'Present Perfect' tense (Have/Has + V3) to talk about the things you have already finished!

contentItems.text: Remember the rules! 'I' takes 'Have'. 'He' takes 'Has'. And you MUST use the third form of the verb (Done, Written, Eaten)!

scenario: Reviewing a daily task list with a roommate.

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Have you cleaned the room?' (क्या तुमने कमरा साफ कर लिया है?) जवाब दें: 'हाँ, मैंने कमरा साफ कर लिया है।' (Yes, I have...)

sampleResponse.targetText: Yes, I have cleaned the room.
sampleResponse.transliteration: येस, आई हैव क्लीन्ड द रूम।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने कमरा साफ कर लिया है।

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Great. And what about your homework? Did you do it?' (बढ़िया। और तुम्हारे होमवर्क के बारे में क्या? क्या तुमने इसे किया?) जवाब दें: 'मैंने अपना होमवर्क कर लिया है।' (I have... done)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: मैंने अपना होमवर्क कर लिया है।

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Where is John? Is he here?' (जॉन कहाँ है? क्या वह यहाँ है?) जवाब दें: 'नहीं, वह जा चुका है।' (No, he has... gone)
अमित: 'Did John eat his food before he went?' (क्या जॉन ने जाने से पहले अपना खाना खाया था?) जवाब दें: 'हाँ, उसने अपना खाना खा लिया है।' (Yes, he has... eaten)

sampleResponse.targetText: Yes, he has eaten his food.
sampleResponse.transliteration: येस, ही हैज़ ईटन हिज़ फूड।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: हाँ, उसने अपना खाना खा लिया है।

subTasks.prompt: अमित: 'Okay, what about the emails? Did you write them?' (ठीक है, ईमेल के बारे में क्या? क्या तुमने उन्हें लिखा?) जवाब दें: 'हाँ, मैंने ईमेल लिख लिए हैं।' (Yes, I have... written)

sampleResponse.targetText: Yes, I have written the emails.
sampleResponse.transliteration: येस, आई हैव रिटन द ईमेल्स।
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने ईमेल लिख लिए हैं।

scenarioContext.baseTranslation: क्या तुमने कमरा साफ कर लिया है?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, I have cleaned the room.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, आई हैव क्लीन्ड द रूम।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने कमरा साफ कर लिया है।
मैंने अपना होमवर्क कर लिया है।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, he has eaten his food.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, ही हैज़ ईटन हिज़ फूड।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, उसने अपना खाना खा लिया है।

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, I have written the emails.
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, आई हैव रिटन द ईमेल्स।
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, मैंने ईमेल लिख लिए हैं।

scoringRubric.V3 Accuracy: Did the user orally produce the correct irregular V3 endings (-en, -ne)? E.g. 'eaten', 'written', 'gone', 'done'?
scoringRubric.Auxiliary Alignment: Did the user shift seamlessly from 'I have' to 'He has' in tasks 3 and 4?
scoringRubric.Base Verb Avoidance: Did the user completely avoid saying 'I have do' or 'He has eat'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Yes, I have cleaned the room. I have done my homework. No, he has gone. Yes, he has eaten his food. Yes, I have written the emails.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You successfully navigated the Present Perfect! You easily switched between 'Have' and 'Has', and you proved you know your irregular V3 verbs (Done, Gone, Eaten, Written)!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! A common mistake is saying 'He has go' or 'I have did'. Remember the formula: Have/Has + Verb 3. The third form of 'Go' is 'Gone'. The third form of 'Do' is 'Done'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! You did great with 'I have', but watch out for 'He'. When talking about John, you MUST say 'He HAS gone', never 'He have gone'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Amit: Have you cleaned the room?... (full dialogue)

hintText: Focus heavily on the final consonant sounds of the V3 verbs! Writ-TEN. Ea-TEN. Go-NE. Do-NE.
commonMistakes: Failing to shift to 'has' for the third person singular 'He'.
